name: Tilt
what_it_does: Kubernetes local development environment tool with live-update, smart rebuilds, and a rich web UI for monitoring multi-service development.
use_cases: Develop multi-service Kubernetes applications locally with automatic container rebuilds on code changes
Monitor all services, logs, and pod statuses in a unified web dashboard during development
Implement fast live_update syncing of code changes without full image rebuild for hot reloading
Script complex local dev workflows in Tiltfile (Starlark) with dependencies between services
Share reproducible local dev environments across team via Tiltfiles in version control
not_for: CI/CD pipelines or production deployments — Tilt is local development only
Teams preferring CLI-only workflows without web UI
Non-Kubernetes projects (though Docker Compose support exists via extension)
best_when: Best for microservices teams who want a visual, fast local Kubernetes dev loop with live reloading.
avoid_when: Avoid for CI/CD or production — use Skaffold or ArgoCD for those workflows.

auth.notes: Uses kubeconfig credentials. Tiltfile configuration is plain Starlark.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error messages shown in Tilt UI and terminal; Starlark config errors are line-referenced.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Tiltfile execution is idempotent — re-running applies same configuration.
known_agent_gotchas: live_update sync rules are path-relative inside container — mismatched paths cause silent no-op updates
Tiltfile is Starlark (not Python) — Python standard library is unavailable; use built-in Tilt functions only
Port-forwarding definitions in Tiltfile override any kubectl port-forward — running both causes port conflicts
Tilt's k8s_resource() function must match exact Kubernetes resource names — partial matches fail silently
CI mode (tilt ci) times out after all resources are ready but does not track runtime errors after initial convergence

security_score_components.security_notes: Tiltfiles can accidentally expose secrets via environment variable injection — review before sharing Tiltfiles.
